evals.name: vaccination-join-timeseries
evals.prompt: I have two CSV files about global COVID vaccination data. The country-level daily data is at evals/data/gpreda--covid-world-vaccination-progress/country_vaccinations.csv and manufacturer-level data is at evals/data/gpreda--covid-world-vaccination-progress/country_vaccinations_by_manufacturer.csv. Build a Deephaven dashboard that joins these two tables on country/location to show vaccination progress by manufacturer for each country. Include a time-series line chart of cumulative vaccinations over time, a bar chart showing manufacturer market share, and a picker to filter by country. Use deephaven.ui.dashboard for the layout.
evals.expected_output: A working dashboard that joins the two CSV files on country, shows time-series trends, manufacturer breakdown, and has a country picker filter.
expectations: The script executes without errors via dh exec
The dashboard renders successfully via dh render
The script reads both CSV files using deephaven.read_csv
The script uses a join operation (natural_join, join, or exact_join) to combine the two tables
The dashboard includes a line chart showing vaccination trends over time
The dashboard includes a picker or filter for country selection
The dashboard uses ui.dashboard with a multi-panel layout
The rendered dashboard contains no panels titled 'Untitled'

evals.name: student-course-join
evals.prompt: I have student performance data split across two files — math course students at evals/data/uciml--student-alcohol-consumption/student-mat.csv and Portuguese course students at evals/data/uciml--student-alcohol-consumption/student-por.csv. Both files share the same schema. Build a Deephaven dashboard that loads both tables, combines them with a join or merge operation, and shows how alcohol consumption (Dalc for weekday, Walc for weekend) correlates with final grades (G3). Include a scatter plot of alcohol vs grades, a bar chart of average grades by alcohol level, and a picker to switch between math and Portuguese students. Use deephaven.ui.dashboard.
evals.expected_output: A dashboard joining the two student datasets, showing alcohol-grade correlations with interactive filtering between courses.
expectations: The script executes without errors via dh exec
The dashboard renders successfully via dh render
The script reads both CSV files
The script combines or compares the two datasets in some way
The dashboard includes a scatter or correlation chart relating alcohol and grades
The dashboard includes aggregated statistics (e.g. avg grades by alcohol level)
The dashboard has a picker or filter to select between math and Portuguese courses
The rendered dashboard contains no panels titled 'Untitled'

evals.name: india-covid-multi-join
evals.prompt: I have three CSVs about COVID in India: case data at evals/data/sudalairajkumar--covid19-in-india/covid_19_india.csv (columns: Date, State/UnionTerritory, Confirmed, Cured, Deaths), testing data at evals/data/sudalairajkumar--covid19-in-india/StatewiseTestingDetails.csv (columns: Date, State, TotalSamples, Negative, Positive), and vaccination data at evals/data/sudalairajkumar--covid19-in-india/covid_vaccine_statewise.csv. Build a Deephaven dashboard that joins the case and testing data by state and date, and shows a state-level overview with: a time-series of confirmed cases, a test positivity rate (positive/total tests), and vaccination progress. Add a picker to filter by state. Use deephaven.ui.dashboard.
evals.expected_output: A dashboard joining three COVID India datasets by state/date, showing case trends, test positivity, and vaccination progress with state filtering.
expectations: The script executes without errors via dh exec
The dashboard renders successfully via dh render
The script reads at least two of the three CSV files
The script performs a join operation on state and/or date columns
The dashboard includes a time-series chart of case data
The dashboard includes a computed metric like test positivity rate
The dashboard has a picker to filter by state
The rendered dashboard contains no panels titled 'Untitled'

evals.name: hotel-monthly-aggregations
evals.prompt: I have hotel booking data at evals/data/jessemostipak--hotel-booking-demand/hotel_bookings.csv with 119k rows. Columns include hotel (Resort/City), is_canceled, lead_time, arrival_date_year, arrival_date_month, stays_in_weekend_nights, stays_in_week_nights, adults, children, babies, meal, country, market_segment, adr, and more. Build a Deephaven dashboard with: a bar chart (dx.bar) of monthly booking counts by hotel type, a bar chart of cancellation rate comparison between Resort and City hotels, a bar chart (dx.bar) of average daily rate (adr) by market_segment (pre-aggregate with agg_by first), and a picker to filter by arrival_date_year. Use agg_by for the aggregations and deephaven.ui.dashboard for layout.
evals.expected_output: A dashboard with monthly booking aggregations, cancellation rates by hotel type, ADR by segment, and year filtering.
expectations: The script executes without errors via dh exec
The dashboard renders successfully via dh render
The script uses agg_by, count_by, or avg_by for computing aggregated statistics
The dashboard shows booking counts broken down by hotel type
The dashboard includes a cancellation rate metric or chart
The dashboard includes a bar chart (dx.bar) of ADR (average daily rate) by market segment
The dashboard has a picker to filter by year
The rendered dashboard contains no panels titled 'Untitled'

evals.name: videogame-sales-ranking
evals.prompt: I have video game sales data at evals/data/gregorut--videogamesales/vgsales.csv with columns: Rank, Name, Platform, Year, Genre, Publisher, NA_Sales, EU_Sales, JP_Sales, Other_Sales, Global_Sales. Build a Deephaven dashboard with: a bar chart (dx.bar) of total sales by genre, a bar chart of total sales by platform, a table of the top 10 publishers by global sales, and a line chart (dx.line) of annual global sales trends over time. Include a picker to filter by genre. Pre-aggregate data with sum_by or agg_by before plotting. Use deephaven.ui.dashboard for layout.
evals.expected_output: A dashboard with bar charts for genre/platform sales, top publishers table, line chart of annual trends, and genre filtering.
expectations: The script executes without errors via dh exec
The dashboard renders successfully via dh render
The script uses aggregation operations (sum_by, agg_by) to compute total sales before plotting
The dashboard includes a bar chart (dx.bar) of sales by genre or platform
The dashboard shows a ranking or top-N publishers by sales
The dashboard includes a line chart (dx.line) of annual sales trends
The dashboard has a picker to filter by genre

evals.name: credit-card-churn-segments

Build a Deephaven dashboard analyzing customer churn: aggregate average transaction amount and credit utilization by Attrition_Flag, show distribution of churned vs existing customers by income category and card category, and add a scatter plot of credit limit vs utilization ratio colored by attrition status. Include a picker to filter by Card_Category. Use agg_by for aggregations.

The dashboard renders successfully via dh render

evals.name: telco-churn-null-handling
evals.prompt: I have telecom customer data at evals/data/blastchar--telco-customer-churn/WA_Fn-UseC_-Telco-Customer-Churn.csv. It has 21 columns including customerID, gender, SeniorCitizen, tenure, PhoneService, InternetService, OnlineSecurity, StreamingTV, Contract, MonthlyCharges, TotalCharges, and Churn. Warning: the TotalCharges column is stored as a string and has some blank/null values that need to be handled carefully when converting to numeric. Build a Deephaven dashboard with: a bar chart (dx.bar) of churn rates by contract type, a histogram (dx.histogram) of MonthlyCharges distribution split by Churn status, and a bar chart of service adoption breakdown. Parse TotalCharges to numeric safely. Include a picker to filter by InternetService type. Use deephaven.ui.dashboard.
evals.expected_output: A dashboard that safely handles the TotalCharges null/string issue and shows churn analysis with service breakdowns.
expectations: The script executes without errors via dh exec
The dashboard renders successfully via dh render
The script executes without crashing on blank TotalCharges values (either by explicit null-safe parsing or by relying on read_csv type inference)
The dashboard shows churn rates broken down by contract type
The dashboard includes a histogram or chart (dx.histogram or dx.bar) comparing monthly charges by churn status
The dashboard has a picker for InternetService type filtering
The rendered dashboard contains no panels titled 'Untitled'

evals.name: customer-personality-tab-delimited
evals.prompt: I have customer data at evals/data/imakash3011--customer-personality-analysis/marketing_campaign.csv. Note: this file uses tab delimiters, not commas. It has columns like ID, Year_Birth, Education, Marital_Status, Income, Kidhome, Teenhome, MntWines, MntFruits, MntMeatProducts, MntFishProducts, MntSweetProducts, MntGoldProds, NumDealsPurchases, NumWebPurchases, NumCatalogPurchases, NumStorePurchases, and campaign response columns (AcceptedCmp1-5, Response). Build a Deephaven dashboard showing: total spending by product category aggregated across customers, purchase channel comparison (web vs catalog vs store), and customer segmentation by education level showing average income and spending. Add a picker to filter by Marital_Status. Handle the tab-delimited format correctly.
evals.expected_output: A dashboard that correctly loads tab-delimited data and shows customer spending analysis with segmentation.
expectations: The script executes without errors via dh exec
The dashboard renders successfully via dh render
The script correctly handles the tab-delimited CSV format

The dashboard includes a comparison of purchase channels
The dashboard shows customer segments by education level
